heading: Presumptions where owner of large amount of cash is not identified
label: 6867 Presumptions where owner of large amount of cash is not identified
notes: Amendments1988—Subsec. (b)(2). substituted “the highest rate of tax specified in section 1” for “a 50-percent rate”.Pub. L. 100–647

children.text: as belonging to another person whose identity the Secretary can readily ascertain and who acknowledges ownership of such cash,
the entire amount of the cash shall be treated as taxable income for the taxable year in which the possession occurs,
such income shall be treated as taxable at the highest rate of tax specified in section 1, and
except as provided in subsection (c), the possessor of the cash shall be treated (solely with respect to such cash) as the taxpayer for purposes of chapters 63 and 64 and section 7429(a)(1).

children.heading: Effect of later substitution of true owner
children.label: c Effect of later substitution of true owner
children.text: If, after an assessment resulting from the application of subsection (a), such assessment is abated and replaced by an assessment against the owner of the cash, such later assessment shall be treated for purposes of all laws relating to lien, levy and collection as relating back to the date of the original assessment.

children.heading: Cash
children.label: 1 Cash
children.text: The term “cash” includes any cash equivalent.

children.label: i
children.text: is of a type which has been frequently used in illegal activities, and

children.label: ii
children.text: is specified as a cash equivalent for purposes of this part in regulations prescribed by the Secretary.

children.label: A
children.text: in the case of a bearer obligation, at its face amount, and

children.label: B
children.text: in the case of any other cash equivalent, at its fair market value.
